rag_rules: Repair only AGREE clauses that directly resolve unresolved identifiers or type mismatches in the current component scope.
Do not modify AADL structure; only adjust AGREE annex content to match visible features and declared types.
generic_rules: Preserve type consistency: use only int, real, bool in AGREE declarations and match them to AADL port/data types.
Reference only visible features or locally declared helpers; never invent identifiers or use package-qualified names not declared in with clauses.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use eq, const, assume, guarantee, assign with correct syntax: name : type = expression;
Component type annexes must not reference subcomponent paths or implementation-only ports.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE variables.
Never add new with clauses or package imports to fix unresolved identifiers—this changes architecture.
irrelevant_topics: component type vs implementation contract design patterns
